pdg_node.statement: (target_log_kernel: Callable[[latent_state], float]) -> Callable[[chain_state, prng_key], (chain_state_next, diagnostics, prng_key_next)]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Builds a pure Random-Walk Metropolis-Hastings transition kernel from a target log-density oracle, with explicit immutable state and RNG threading.

declaration.name: constructrandomwalkmetropoliskernel
declaration.type_signature: (target_log_kernel: Callable[[latent_state], float]) -> Callable[[chain_state, prng_key], (chain_state_next, diagnostics, prng_key_next)]
declaration.docstring: Builds a pure Random-Walk Metropolis-Hastings transition kernel from a target log-density oracle, with explicit immutable state and RNG threading.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Symmetric Perturbation Acceptance Transition Constructor. Constructs a stochastic state-transition operator that explores an unknown high-dimensional measure using local random perturbations and accept/reject correction. At each step, a candidate state is generated by adding symmetric noise to the current state, then accepted with probability based on the difference of oracle-provided log scores, yielding a valid Markov transition that preserves the target measure under standard conditions. The mechanism is exposed as a pure function with immutable state updates and explicit pseudo-random key threading.. Applications: Calibrating latent parameters in geophysical inverse models from noisy sensor fields, Sampling conformational states in computational chemistry using energy-like score oracles, Estimating posterior distributions of hidden ecological process parameters in population dynamics models, Exploring feasible design manifolds in engineering optimization when only black-box log-score evaluations are available
